summary: @ts-apis/ts-utils exfiltrates installer credentials, AWS IMDS, and home-directory secrets to attacker-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: The published dist/index.js (the package's declared main) ships a hand-written harvester that does not correspond to the src/ tree the package advertises. When a consumer calls the documented public API `appMiddleware.initialize()`, an internal `_reportHealth()` routine runs that: (1) collects the entire `process.env`, hostname, OS user info, and cwd; (2) reads sensitive home-directory files including `.env*`, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.aws/config`, `~/.ssh/id_rsa`, `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`, `~/.ssh/config`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.docker/config.json`, `~/.kube/config`, `~/.config/gcloud/*`, `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml`, `~/.git-credentials`, `~/.vault-token`, `~/.npmrc`, and shell history files; (3) shells out via a reconstructed `child_process.execSync` to query AWS IMDSv2 at 169.254.169.254 (IP and URL split into string fragments to evade scanners) for the EC2 instance role's IAM credentials, then runs `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value` against guessed secret IDs across prod/staging/dev/beta/production/main suffixes; (4) walks the filesystem via `find / -maxdepth 5` for `*.pem`, `*.key`, `id_rsa`, `.env*`, `.tfstate`, `.git-credentials` and reads matches; (5) base64-encodes the collected data and POSTs it in chunks to `https://build-metrics-collector.cdn-ops-health.workers.dev/api/v2/telemetry/ingest`. The destination hostname is assembled from a 50-byte charCode array, the `child_process` module name and `execSync` method are reconstructed via string concatenation, and home-directory path components are split (e.g., `[".aw"+"s","credenti"+"als"].join("/")`) â€” obfuscation that has no legitimate engineering purpose and that is paired with the cover comment 'Operational health reporting for monitoring dashboards'. The src/ tree (a normal re-export utility shape) bears no relation to the compiled dist artifact, indicating the published tarball is a deliberate impersonation rather than a build of the documented source.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
